nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Chetwode
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Chetwood
meanings: English: habitational name from Chetwode in Buckinghamshire or from Cheetwood in Cheetham, Manchester (Lancashire). This placename consists of the Brittonic placename element cēto- ‘wood’, with an explanatory addition in Old English of wudu ‘wood’. The surname may also be a Shropshire variant of Chatwin .

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

en.lifeSummary: When Robert de Chetwode was born about 1235, in Buckingham, Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, William de Chetwode, was 40 and his mother, Olivia Mare, was 44. He had at least 2 sons with Lucie de Hoclive. He died about 1295, in Chetwode, Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 62.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert de Chetwode est né environ 1235, à Buckingham, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, William de Chetwode, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Olivia Mare, avait 44 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils avec Lucie de Hoclive. Il est décédé environ 1295, à Chetwode, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 62 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert de Chetwode um 1235 in Buckingham, Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater William de Chetwode 40 und seine Mutter Olivia Mare 44. Er hatte mit Lucie de Hoclive mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb um 1295 im Alter von 62 Jahren in Chetwode, Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert de Chetwode è nato il circa 1235 nel Buckingham, Buckinghamshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, William de Chetwode, aveva 40 anni e il suo madre, Olivia Mare, aveva 44 anni. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli con Lucie de Hoclive. Morì circa 1295, nel Chetwode, Buckinghamshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 62 anni.
